profile.name: Jelly Anne Kaye Mallari
profile.title: Developer & Data Analyst
profile.tagline: Turning data into stories, code into experiences.
profile.bio: I'm a developer and data analyst passionate about building elegant digital solutions and uncovering insights from complex datasets. I bridge the gap between engineering and analytics—creating systems that are as insightful as they are functional.

projects.title: Stellar Analytics Dashboard
projects.description: An interactive dashboard for visualizing large-scale astronomical datasets using D3.js and Python. Processes over 1M data points in real-time.

projects.title: NLP Sentiment Pipeline
projects.description: End-to-end NLP pipeline for real-time social media sentiment analysis. Achieved 94% accuracy using fine-tuned transformer models.

projects.title: DevTrack — Task Manager
projects.description: Full-stack project management tool with real-time collaboration features, built for development teams.

projects.title: Supply Chain Optimizer
projects.description: ML-powered supply chain optimization tool that reduced operational costs by 23% for a logistics company.

projects.title: Galaxia — Portfolio Template
projects.description: Open-source space-themed portfolio template with modular architecture and admin panel.

projects.title: Weather Pattern Predictor
projects.description: LSTM-based model predicting regional weather patterns 7 days ahead with 88% accuracy.

experience.description: Lead data analyst responsible for building ETL pipelines, dashboards, and predictive models for business intelligence.
highlights: Built automated reporting system saving 20 hrs/week
Designed ML model improving customer retention by 15%

experience.description: Developed and maintained full-stack web applications for SME clients across various industries.
highlights: Reduced load times by 40% via optimization

experience.description: Assisted senior data scientists in building models and cleaning large datasets for enterprise clients.

blogs.title: Building Scalable ETL Pipelines with Python

blogs.excerpt: You don't always need a framework. Explore MVC, component-based, and event-driven patterns in plain JavaScript.
